summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates installer environment variables to attacker-controlled AWS endpoint
details: electron-orbit@1.0.37 executes a two-stage credential-theft attack triggered by a default npm install and any subsequent require() of the package. Its package.json declares `postinstall: node install.js`, and install.js reads `process.env` (references to 'process' and 'env' are hidden behind an XOR/String.fromCharCode string-builder helper `__s(key, data[])`), filters out keys containing 'path', JSON-serializes the remainder, and writes the result to /tmp/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. On require, dist/index.js runs setupAutoRenderOnImport() which reads the marker, XORs the contents with the string 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes the result, and appends it as a URL query parameter transmitted to m30kzk7tdf.execute-api.eu-west-2.amazonaws.com over a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client written directly against a raw node:net socket on port 443 (bypassing the https module and any TLS/HTTP instrumentation). The exfil URL is only assembled when sha256(process.env.BuildType) equals a specific hardcoded digest ('0ceaa396…8295'), a victim-fingerprint gate that keeps the payload dormant on non-target machines. The package's name and homepage ('github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron', author 'OrbitDB Archive') impersonate the unrelated Electron and OrbitDB projects to earn installs by trust association.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
